steps.title: Identify the causal mechanism in the notes
steps.content: The student's goal is to identify what causes meanders. The decisive clue appears in the notes: “A meander forms when water erodes sediment from one side of the riverbank and redeposits that sediment on the opposite side.”

steps.title: Find which choice states this mechanism
steps.content: Choice D states that erosion and redeposition of sediment forms meanders. Choices A and B describe properties of meanders without explaining causation, and C reverses the relationship. That matches choice D — D is correct.
